steps.title: Calculate the confidence interval from margin of error
steps.content: The margin of error defines the range where the true population value plausibly lies. We calculate: lower bound = $8.5\% - 2.73\% = 5.77\%$ and upper bound = $8.5\% + 2.73\% = 11.23\%$.

steps.title: Interpret the interval to identify the correct answer
steps.content:
The interval [5.77%, 11.23%] represents plausible values for the true defect rate. (A) incorrectly identifies 2.73% as the defect percentage. (C) gives only the point estimate 8.5%, ignoring uncertainty. (D) claims values above 11.23% are plausible, contradicting the upper bound.

The correct conclusion is that it is plausible the true defect rate is between 5.77% and 11.23%.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
